description_long: This is a small, single-engine airplane with a high-wing configuration and a tricycle landing gear. The fuselage is primarily white with red accents along the nose, wingtips, tail, and undercarriage, giving it a classic general aviation appearance. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and features a dark, enclosed canopy with side windows, suggesting a two- or four-seat arrangement. The wings are straight and mounted above the fuselage, supported by struts, and the tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The propeller is mounted at the nose, indicating a piston engine. The overall design is typical of light utility or training aircraft commonly seen at small airports or airfields near urban areas.
description: A small, high-wing, single-engine airplane with a white and red color scheme, tricycle landing gear, and a conventional tail. The cockpit is enclosed and positioned at the front, with a nose-mounted propeller.
description_view_0: Side view showing the left profile, highlighting the high-mounted wing, red accents, and the single propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full wingspan, nose-mounted propeller, and the tricycle landing gear arrangement.
description_view_2: Side view from the right, similar to view 0 but with the tail number visible and the right wing in focus.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail section, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the main landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles; supported by struts.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with side windows, likely single or dual seat.
